title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Miss Blacklock and Dora Bunner continue to discuss the newspaper notice regarding a murder scheduled for six-thirty that evening. While Miss Bunner finds the announcement frightening and dangerous, Miss Blacklock maintains her stance that it is merely a joke. Their conversation is interrupted by Miki, a moody household helper and European refugee. Miki, claiming her family was killed by enemies, expresses a desperate desire to leave the area, believing that Nazis or Bolsheviks are coming to kill her. She cites the newspaper notice as evidence of this impending danger. Miss Blacklock attempts to calm her by suggesting they prepare beef and cheese straws, noting that people will be arriving at six-thirty for a "funeral."

Later, in the drawing room of the Victorian-style house, Miss Blacklock prepares for the arrival of guests by arranging flowers, a tablecloth, and drinks. Patrick and Julia Simmons join her, leading to a discussion regarding the scarcity of coal and the differences between the past and the present. Miss Bunner contributes to the conversation by recalling their shared history and mentioning that Letty was once a highly educated secretary. Felicia Hyms enters the room, surprised by the gathering. Patrick dramatically announces that the room is a "crime scene," explaining that the flowers are wreaths and the food is intended for a funeral feast. When Felicia and Philippa Hammers express confusion and ask if it is a joke, Miss Blacklock instructs them to show Felicia the notice before she goes to tend to the ducks.
